factors.summary: The company continues to return value to shareholders through consistent dividend payments.

evidence.text: During the three months ended March 31, 2023 and 2022, we declared aggregate dividends of $30 million [...] On May 2, 2023, we announced a dividend of $0.035 per share to holders of Common Stock and Class B Common Stock of record on June 15, 2023, which will be distributed on July 6, 2023.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is relatively stable, with slight fluctuations in operating and investing activities.

evidence.text: Cash provided by operating activities remained relatively flat, as compared to the prior period, and primarily included: [...] The remainder of the activity related to changes in assets and liabilities due to the volume and timing of other operating cash receipts and payments with respect to when the transactions are reflected in earnings.

factors.summary: The company's market share in the Solar segment is under pressure due to lower installation revenues and increased competition.

evidence.text: During the three months ended March 31, 2023, the decrease as compared to the prior period, was primarily due to lower installation revenue, net of the associated costs, of $43 million, partially offset by lower SG&A.

factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices, contributing to revenue growth.

evidence.text: During the three months ended March 31, 2023, the increases in revenue, as compared to the prior period, included: • Monitoring and related services revenue: higher recurring revenue of $35 million, reflecting approximately $31 million related to the estimated impact from an increase in average prices and approximately $8 million related to the estimated impact from an increase in subscribers, partially offset by a decrease of $4 million in other revenue primarily due to lower radio conversion revenue.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, particularly in CSB and Commercial, driven by an increase in outright sales transactions.

evidence.text: As we continue to build our partnership with Google, introduce new or enhance our current offerings, and refine our go-to-market approach, we expect to see a shift toward an increasing proportion of outright sales transactions in our CSB and Commercial segments, which will impact results in future periods when those changes occur.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, particularly in CSB and Commercial segments, driven by higher sales and installations.

evidence.text: During the three months ended March 31, 2023, the increases in revenue, as compared to the prior period, included: • Security installation, product, and other: an increase in the amortization of deferred subscriber acquisition revenue of $18 million as a result of a higher population of existing customers under a Company-owned model, as well as an increase in installation revenue of $16 million primarily driven by a higher volume of outright sales transactions.

factors.summary: The Commercial segment is growing fastest, driven by strong sales performance and improved average revenue per subscriber.

evidence.text: During the three months ended March 31, 2023, the increases in revenue, as compared to the prior period, included: • M&S Revenue: (i) higher revenue from time and materials billings of $11 million driven by higher revenue per service call, as well as (ii) higher recurring revenue of $5 million driven by improvements in average revenue per subscriber.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising, particularly in materials and labor, but the company is partially offsetting these through pricing and cost-saving initiatives.

evidence.text: We may experience an increase in costs associated with factors such as (i) offering a wider variety of products and services; (ii) providing a greater mix of interactive and smart home solutions; (iii) replacing or upgrading certain system components or technology due to technological advancements, cybersecurity upgrades, or otherwise; (iv) supply chain disruptions; (v) inflationary pressures on costs such as materials, labor, and fuel; and (vi) other changes in prices, interest rates, or terms from our suppliers, vendors, or third-party lenders. Changes in interest rates or terms from third-party lenders that provide loan products to our Solar customers are impacted by factors such as the Federal Reserve increasing the risk-free interest rate or other developments in the financial markets.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving through initiatives like the Virtual Assistance Program, which reduces costs by eliminating thousands of vehicle trips daily.

evidence.text: Advances in technology are also helping us to improve our products and services and reduce our costs. For example, our innovative virtual service support program (the “Virtual Assistance Program”), which we launched for our residential customers in July 2021, provides our customers the ability to troubleshoot and resolve certain service issues through a live video stream with our skilled technicians. This provides customers with more options for receiving certain services that best fit their lifestyles while reducing the cost for us to provide these services and lowering our carbon footprint by eliminating thousands of vehicle trips each day.

factors.summary: Management appears cautious due to macroeconomic conditions but confident in their ability to offset costs through pricing and efficiency.

evidence.text: As part of our response to changes or pressures in the macroeconomic environment, we may evaluate cost saving opportunities such as reducing headcount or our physical facilities footprint when appropriate. While we have experienced some increase in costs as a result of inflation, we have, for the most part, been able to offset the rising costs through price increases to our customers, as well as cost saving opportunities.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include macroeconomic factors such as inflation, supply chain disruptions, and higher non-payment disconnects.

evidence.text: During 2022 and through the first quarter of 2023, we experienced fewer relocation disconnects and higher non-pay disconnects largely related to housing market conditions and the weaker macroeconomic environment. We may continue to experience fluctuations in these or other trends in the future as changes in the general macroeconomic environment or housing market develop.

factors.summary: The long-term strategic direction includes expanding partnerships, introducing new products, and enhancing customer experience through innovation.

evidence.text: As we continue to build our partnership with Google, introduce new or enhance our current offerings, and refine our go-to-market approach, we expect to see a shift toward an increasing proportion of outright sales transactions in our CSB and Commercial segments, which will impact results in future periods when those changes occur.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include inflationary pressures, supply chain disruptions, and changes in interest rates.

evidence.text: We may experience an increase in costs associated with factors such as (i) offering a wider variety of products and services; (ii) providing a greater mix of interactive and smart home solutions; (iii) replacing or upgrading certain system components or technology due to technological advancements, cybersecurity upgrades, or otherwise; (iv) supply chain disruptions; (v) inflationary pressures on costs such as materials, labor, and fuel; and (vi) other changes in prices, interest rates, or terms from our suppliers, vendors, or third-party lenders. Changes in interest rates or terms from third-party lenders that provide loan products to our Solar customers are impacted by factors such as the Federal Reserve increasing the risk-free interest rate or other developments in the financial markets.

factors.summary: A key catalyst is the introduction of the ADT+ app, expected by the end of 2023, which could enhance customer acquisition and retention.

evidence.text: During the first quarter of 2023, we introduced our new ADT+ app for our self-setup line of do-it-yourself (“DIY”) smart home security products, including Google Nest offerings, which we expect to introduce for professional installations by the end of 2023.

factors.summary: The company's competitive differentiation is strong through its partnership with Google and the introduction of innovative products like the ADT+ app.

evidence.text: As we continue to build our partnership with Google, introduce new or enhance our current offerings, and refine our go-to-market approach, we expect to see a shift toward an increasing proportion of outright sales transactions in our CSB and Commercial segments, which will impact results in future periods when those changes occur.

factors.summary: Interest rate increases are negatively impacting the company through higher borrowing costs and reduced demand for Solar products.

evidence.text: The increase in interest expense, net was driven by unrealized losses on our interest rate swaps not designated as cash flow hedges of $33 million during the three months ended March 31, 2023 compared to unrealized gains of $145 million during the three months ended March 31, 2022. The increase also reflects higher interest expense of $27 million related to our First Lien Term Loan due 2026 offset by a decrease of $33 million related to settlements on our interest rate swaps.

factors.summary: Mergers and acquisitions are focused on strategic optimization, particularly in the Solar segment, with associated restructuring costs.

evidence.text: During the three months ended March 31, 2023, merger, restructuring, integration, and other includes restructuring costs primarily related to certain facility exits, as well as integration and third-party costs related to the strategic optimization of our Solar business operations following the acquisition of ADT Solar.

factors.summary: The company is investing in digital technologies, such as the Virtual Assistance Program, to improve efficiency and reduce costs.

evidence.text: Advances in technology are also helping us to improve our products and services and reduce our costs. For example, our innovative virtual service support program (the “Virtual Assistance Program”), which we launched for our residential customers in July 2021, provides our customers the ability to troubleshoot and resolve certain service issues through a live video stream with our skilled technicians. This provides customers with more options for receiving certain services that best fit their lifestyles while reducing the cost for us to provide these services and lowering our carbon footprint by eliminating thousands of vehicle trips each day.

factors.summary: New product launches, such as the ADT+ app, are expected to contribute to future growth and customer retention.

evidence.text: During the first quarter of 2023, we introduced our new ADT+ app for our self-setup line of do-it-yourself (“DIY”) smart home security products, including Google Nest offerings, which we expect to introduce for professional installations by the end of 2023.

factors.summary: Regulatory changes, such as the Inflation Reduction Act, are favorable for the Solar business by extending tax credits, which could boost demand.

evidence.text: Under the Inflation Reduction Act (“IRA”), the Investment Tax Credit (“ITC”) was extended until 2032 to allow a qualifying homeowner to deduct 30% of the cost of installing residential solar systems from their U.S. federal income taxes. Under the current terms, the ITC will remain at 30% through the end of 2032 and be further reduced in increments down to 0.0% after the end of 2034, unless extended. We believe this incentive could be favorable for our Solar business.

factors.summary: The company is facing margin pressure primarily from impairment charges and cost increases, particularly in the Solar segment, but is taking measures to mitigate these impacts.

evidence.text: Based on the results of our interim impairment analysis, we recorded a non-cash impairment charge of $193 million during the first quarter of 2023. Following the impairment loss, the remaining balance of goodwill attributable to our Solar reporting unit is approximately $370 million.

The increase in interest expense, net was driven by unrealized losses on our interest rate swaps not designated as cash flow hedges of $33 million during the three months ended March 31, 2023 compared to unrealized gains of $145 million during the three months ended March 31, 2022. The increase also reflects higher interest expense of $27 million related to our First Lien Term Loan due 2026 offset by a decrease of $33 million related to settlements on our interest rate swaps.

factors.summary: The company has implemented cost reduction measures, including restructuring and impairment charges, particularly in Solar, but these savings are partially offset by rising costs in other areas such as amortization of deferred subscriber acquisition costs.

evidence.text: We recorded a non-cash impairment charge of $193 million during the first quarter of 2023.

During the three months ended March 31, 2023, the decrease in selling, general, and administrative expenses ('SG&A'), as compared to the prior period, was primarily driven by: • a decrease of $18 million in Solar SG&A, primarily due to recent cost reduction initiatives, including lower selling and advertising costs, • a decrease of $17 million in radio conversion costs due to the wind down of the replacement program, and • a decrease of $14 million in CSB advertising costs, primarily due to utilization of a portion of the Google Success Funds, partially offset by • an increase of $10 million in the amortization of deferred subscriber acquisition costs, primarily related to CSB.
evidence.subsection: Solar: / Selling, General, and Administrative Expenses:

factors.summary: Demand trends show mixed performance across different segments. While the Solar segment experienced a deceleration in demand, other segments such as Monitoring and Security saw slight increases in revenue.

evidence.text: During the three months ended March 31, 2023, the decrease as compared to the prior period, was primarily due to lower installation revenue, net of the associated costs, of $43 million, partially offset by lower SG&A.

Three Months Ended March 31, (in thousands, except as otherwise indicated) 2023 2022 $ Change Revenue: Monitoring and related services $ 1,173,318 $ 1,121,296 $ 52,022 Security installation, product, and other 294,201 231,470 62,731 Solar installation, product, and other 144,835 191,981 (47,146) Total revenue 1,612,354 1,544,747 67,607

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is mixed, with acceleration across most segments except for Solar, which experienced a significant decline.

evidence.text: Organic revenue growth is decelerating, with the Solar segment experiencing a significant decline.

Three Months Ended March 31, (in thousands) 2023 2022 $ Change CSB: Monitoring and related services $ 1,028,634 $ 993,028 $ 35,606 Security installation, product, and other 103,842 69,561 34,281 Total CSB 1,132,476 1,062,589 69,887 Commercial: Monitoring and related services 144,684 128,268 16,416 Security installation, product, and other 190,359 161,909 28,450 Total Commercial 335,043 290,177 44,866 Solar: Solar installation, product, and other 144,835 191,981 (47,146) Total Solar 144,835 191,981 (47,146) Total revenue $ 1,612,354 $ 1,544,747 $ 67,607

factors.summary: The supply chain health is showing signs of improvement, particularly with fewer bulk purchases and more efficient resource use. However, delays continue to impact certain areas within the Commercial segment, affecting costs.

evidence.text: The decrease in cash used in investing activities for the three months ended March 31, 2023, as compared to the prior period, was primarily due to: [...] a decrease in cash paid for dealer generated customer account and bulk account purchases of $69 million primarily related to fewer bulk purchases, as well as [...] a decrease in subscriber system assets expenditures of $23 million due to fewer adds.

During the three months ended March 31, 2023, the increase in cost of revenue, as compared to the prior period, was primarily attributable to an increase in installations and services performed in connection with strong sales performance as discussed above.

factors.summary: The company is increasing its debt leverage through new term loans and amended credit facilities, but this comes with concerns related to significant deferred tax assets and potential valuation allowances that could impact the balance sheet.

evidence.text: In March 2023, we issued an aggregate principal amount of $600 million of term loans under the Term Loan A Facility. [...] In March 2023, we amended the agreement governing the Receivables Facility, pursuant to which, among other things, the borrowing capacity was increased from $400 million to $500 million and the uncommitted revolving period was extended from May 2023 to March 2024.
evidence.subsection: Long-Term Debt / Term Loan A Facility

evidence.text: We have a significant amount of deferred tax assets, against which we take valuation allowances that relate to the uncertainty of our ability to utilize these deferred tax assets in future periods. We review periodically those matters that can influence our decision as to whether or not a valuation allowance is appropriate. Among those matters considered are pending and enacted legislation. We will consider each quarter whether any developments to such legislation, together with the other factors we consider, require a valuation allowance.

factors.summary: Economic conditions have emerged as a significant challenge, primarily manifesting as rising interest rates, inflation, and a weaker macroeconomic environment. These factors have negatively impacted the Solar segment's performance, led to higher non-payment disconnects, and created headwinds for the company's overall operations.

evidence.text: During the first quarter of 2023, due to the at-risk nature of the goodwill attributed to our Solar reporting unit following a goodwill impairment charge during the third quarter of 2022, and as a result of current macroeconomic conditions, including the impact of rising interest rates and financial market conditions on the Company’s third party lenders and customer demand, as well as ADT Solar’s underperformance of operating results in the first quarter relative to expectations, we identified a triggering event related to our Solar reporting unit.

During 2022 and through the first quarter of 2023, we experienced fewer relocation disconnects and higher non-pay disconnects largely related to housing market conditions and the weaker macroeconomic environment. We may continue to experience fluctuations in these or other trends in the future as changes in the general macroeconomic environment or housing market develop.
